previousFix.description: Owner: 'plan and do 3 options and you choose best one'. The coach chat had no character (just a name + 'Personal Coach' = the robot feeling). I prototyped 3 styles on the dark chat at header+FAB sizes -> (A) illustrated bust, (B) calm mascot blob, (C) monogram aura -> and CHOSE A: a drawn human coach (warm, no robot, readable at 40px and 120px); the mascot reads as a generic app blob and the monogram is just a letter. Built CoachAvatar (inline SVG, no external assets): a head-and-shoulders coach drawn deterministically per coach name (skin/hair/accent/hairstyle/optional beard, so every roster coach is a distinct person), with calm idle animation (slow breathing + occasional blink) that respects reduced-motion. Wired into the chat header. Verified live on light AND dark; 'Atlas' vs 'Rohan' render as different people; 0 console errors. Remaining surfaces (quick follow-ups): message name-chip, floating coach button, CoachPreview. If you'd rather use hand-drawn Lottie art per coach, CoachAvatar is the drop-in swap point (spec: docs/COACH_CHARACTER_ASSET_SPEC.md).

previousFix.description: Owner: 'plan and do 3 options, you choose the best'; reopen: the drawn coach 'looks plain and less detailed', wants 'rich and badass'. Root cause: portraits came from a semi-flat/soft-studio prompt that read flat. I generated 3 rich style variants for the test coach — (A) painterly digital painting, (B) stylized 3D render, (C) bold editorial illustration — and CHOSE C: it reads most clearly as a DRAWN character (no robot/stock feel), is the boldest and most detailed, and stays premium (no neon/grunge). Regenerated all 13 active roster coaches in this style (backend generator 54a309b; OpenAI used only for character creation per your constraint). Image 1: the full rich portrait. Image 2: it live in the coach-chat header (drawn Rohan in the animated halo).

(2) What it costs after the trial: the 30-days-free card now shows 'After your free month 499/mo' + the founder offer (49 first month, cancel anytime, quarterly/half-yearly save more). previousFix.description: Re-fix for 'don't stick with one coach rohan — different styles and personality.' The build-plan coach hero already loads the assigned coach's real PORTRAIT, and the coach is NOT hardcoded: app/services/coach_roster.py deterministically matches each user to one of 12 seeded roster coaches (each with its own art + bio), so different users get different coaches (a given user keeps theirs — by design). The gap was that the hero only showed a name, so the personality didn't come through. Fix: CoachDetails now carries bio/specialty (backend already returned them) and the hero renders the matched coach's bio as an italic personality line under the portrait — e.g. Rohan now reads 'Former cricketer turned coach — home-workout progressions and high-protein Indian meals are his lane.' The real coach also loads over any stale cached name. Verified on /dev/coach-preview: portrait + name + personality line render, 0 console errors. (If you want a LARGER visual-style range, the next step is generating more roster coaches/art via the one-time OpenAI pipeline — say the word and I'll batch it.)

previousFix.description: Root cause: the plan-ready 'Plan Details > Meals' list mapped previewDietDay.meals directly (unfiltered), so an unfilled slot (0 ingredients) rendered as 'Dinner ~0 kcal' — unlike the weekly-progression list + the save path, which both strip empty meals. Fix: previewDietDay now filters out meals with no ingredients at the source, so a ~0 kcal empty slot never shows (and if a day has only empty slots, the existing 'No meals added yet' note shows). Verified on /dev/plan-builder?stage=overview (its seed has an empty dinner): Meals now reads just 'Oats Bowl / Chicken Breast Bowl' — the Dinner ~0 kcal row is gone.

previousFix.description: Root cause: each Today's Nutrition meal card showed the meal NAME and a meal-type pill with the same word — 'Breakfast' + a 'Breakfast' pill, 'Lunch' + 'Lunch', 'Afternoon Snack' + 'Snack' (the circled areas) — duplicated text that read as clutter. Fix (NutritionMealCard.tsx): the meal-type chip now renders only when it adds information; when the meal name already contains the type label it's hidden. Result (see shot): cards read cleanly as name + macros — Breakfast / Mid-Morning Snack / Lunch / Afternoon Snack / Evening Snack, no repeated pill. A recipe-named meal (e.g. 'Soy Bowl' in the Dinner slot) still shows its 'Dinner' chip since that's not redundant. Verified on /nutrition, 0 console errors.

Verified on /nutrition: checkboxes read smaller/cleaner and still toggle (tapped Breakfast → checked, totals update), 0 console errors. The redundant meal-type pills from the first pass stay hidden, so each card now reads as a clean name + macros + compact checkbox.

previousFix.description: Re: 'missing image and duplicate images, and the meal title can be over the checkbox.' Fixed 2 of 3 in the frontend (verified on dev): (1) MISSING IMAGE — a meal whose server image is a degenerate ~1x1 dud (e.g. Lunch's 9162.png) used to 'load' fine so onError never fired and the card showed a blank box; AsyncImage now treats naturalWidth<=2 as a failure and falls back to a real meal-photo placeholder (Lunch now shows a proper photo, see shot). (2) TITLE OVER CHECKBOX — added min-w-0 to the title button + break-words to the title so a long meal name stays in its column and can't run under the eaten-checkbox. (3) DUPLICATE IMAGES — the two snack cards are DIFFERENT files (9161 vs 9163), not a frontend dup; they're near-identical AI-generated photos, so that's a backend image-generation/dedup matter (the generator gave two snacks look-alike images) — flagging separately since the FE can't dedup distinct URLs. Ships to your device with the next app version.

issues.description: Can you create the images exercises make sure the prompt is nailed 100% to match the exercise using openai

fix.description: Doing it. Scoped to the 97 exercises that appear in ACTIVE user plans (what people actually see) — cost-conscious vs the full ~2600-row catalog. The generator (now with an ACTIVE_PLANS scope, backend ac41a49) is running on the server, producing clean premium instructional illustrations that match each exercise — sample attached: a banded step-up with accurate form + equipment, flat editorial style, no scraped clickbait. Images go live on the catalog endpoint as each one finishes (the app's AsyncImage picks them up — no app version needed). A few exercises whose prompt trips OpenAI's content filter are skipped and keep their existing image (no regression). Say the word and I'll run the remaining ~1600 catalog exercises for full coverage.

issues.description: These badges should be shown on all screens when achieved not only on the profile page also fot the level up badge this is application wide not just profile page limited

history.note: this cant be before login, how are you wining a badge before login ?

previousFix.description: Root cause: the badge-unlock toast (useBadgeCheck) only ran on the Profile level card and the Achievements gallery, so an earned badge surfaced only when the user opened those two screens. Level-ups were already global via LevelUpWatcher but badges were not. Fix: added BadgeWatcher, mounted once at the app shell beside LevelUpWatcher, which runs the badge check on navigation and on app/tab resume (throttled to 60s) so a newly-earned badge raises its calm achievement toast on ANY screen. The server check is idempotent (it skips already-earned badges) so running it app-wide never repeats a toast. Verified: the badge check now fires on non-Profile screens and the real shipped BadgeWatcher to useBadgeCheck to AchievementProvider chain renders the toast off-Profile (capture attached; the logged-out background is a headless-harness session artifact, not a product state). Client-side change, so it appears on device with the next app version cut from the main workstation.

issues.description: Why do we have some extra space here ?

history.note: no images

previousFix.description: The extra space is the floating-coach-FAB clearance reserved below the last card. Your installed build reserves the old footprint (--fab-clearance 112px, so .pb-fab = 72px nav + 112px + safe-area = ~184px of dead space below the list — matching your screenshot). Current code already sets --fab-clearance to 0, so the reservation collapses to nav-only: verified on the dev build that .pb-fab padding is now 72px (just the bottom-nav height), down from ~184px, so the list ends just above the nav (the coach FAB self-hides at scroll-bottom, so it needs no reserved footprint). CSS-only change, already in the codebase — reaches your device with the next app version. Note: your shot is the gym workout list; the QA test account is walking-only, so the attached capture shows the Activity walking state, but this clearance fix is global to every Activity state. If any residual gap remains after the next version, flag it and I will chase the specific element.

previousFix.description: Done: the coach is now aware of the user's gamification + progress and uses it to motivate. The coach context already carried streak + recent badges + social connections; added the missing LEVEL/XP (GamificationProfile) to the [PROGRESS — STREAK, LEVEL & BADGES] block and instruct it to celebrate level-ups + recent badges (never invent any). Verified live on /coach-chat — asked 'How am I doing with my progress so far?' and the coach replied referencing the real data: 'you've already got your first meal and first check-in badges, which means the restart has begun…' plus streak + meals logged. Backend deployed, so this is live now.

Showing it working (live now, backend). The coach context now includes the user's level/XP + recent badges + streak + social. Demo in the shot — I asked the coach 'How am I doing with my progress so far?' and it replied using the real data: 'you've already got your first meal and first check-in badges, which means the restart has begun' + current streak + meals logged. So when there's progress to mention, the coach surfaces it to motivate. Try it on-device: open the coach and ask about your progress.

previousFix.description: The coach now references the user's badges, progress, streak and social context to motivate. The context already fed the coach this (streak/level/badges, friends/groups/leaderboard, recent DMs + group messages — all wired into ContextAssembler.build), but the prompt flatly banned levels/XP/game-vocabulary, which suppressed it. Reconciled the prompt: the coach may reference rank/level name + badges + streak + social to motivate (calm and concrete — no XP counters, no game emojis), plus a proactive instruction to weave a fresh badge/streak/social win into one calm line. Verified LIVE (screenshot): asked How
